strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was clearly supportive|market/sector environment was clearly supportive|price structure was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-15 24.5
decision_reasoning: GME offers the cleanest risk-adjusted earnings setup in the slate. Trend is modestly positive across all windows (+2.7% 5d, +9.9% 20d), relative strength is positive vs both SPY and sector, and volume is near average. The 25-strike May-15 call is ATM with a tight spread, strong volume, and IV that is elevated but not extreme (~51%), leaving room for directional P&L without catastrophic vol crush. MP's trend is stronger but its 125% IV makes long premium into earnings a poor expected-value trade (explicitly flagged in rejected_contract_reasons). BROS has wide spreads and missing greeks on the near strike; SHAK's lone long-put has a 33% spread and no IV data. GME scores in the Standard recommend band — supportive but not overwhelming setup with a clean contract.
